<pr_description>
REST helper config: `onResponse` provided in config does not work
I am using default REST helper for API testing, and I want to measure and assert the API's response time. For that I am trying following.

In my `codecept.conf.ts`:
```ts
  helpers: {
    REST: {
      endpoint: 'https://myServerUrl',

      onRequest: (request) => {
        console.log('onRequest');
        request.headers[ 'start-time' ] = new Date().getTime();
      },

      onResponse: (response) => {
        console.log('onResponse');
        const startTime = response.request.headers[ 'start-time' ];
        const endTime = new Date().getTime();
        const responseTime = endTime - startTime;
        response. Headers[ 'x-response-time' ] = responseTime;
      },
    },

    JSONResponse: {}
  }
```

and then in my custom helper:
```ts
    const response = this.helpers.JSONResponse.response;
    
    console.log(`Response time: ${response.headers[ 'x-response-time' ]} ms`);
```

I can see that `onRequest` from REST config works as expected but `onResponse` does not work.

My output:
```sh
onRequest
Response time: undefined ms
```

</pr_description>

    16	 * @prop {number} [timeout=1000] - timeout for requests in milliseconds. 10000ms by default.
    17	 * @prop {object} [defaultHeaders] - a list of default headers.
    18	 * @prop {object} [httpAgent] - create an agent with SSL certificate
    19	 * @prop {function} [onRequest] - an async function which can update request object.
    20	 * @prop {function} [onResponse] - an async function which can update response object.
    21	 * @prop {number} [maxUploadFileSize] - set the max content file size in MB when performing api calls.
    22	 */
    23	const config = {}
    24	
    25	/**
    26	 * REST helper allows to send additional requests to the REST API during acceptance tests.
    27	 * [Axios](https://github.com/axios/axios) library is used to perform requests.
    28	 *
    29	 * <!-- configuration -->
    30	 *
    31	 * ## Example
    32	 *
    33	 * ```js
    34	 *{
    35	 *   helpers: {
    36	 *     REST: {
    37	 *       endpoint: 'http://site.com/api',
    38	 *       prettyPrintJson: true,
    39	 *       onRequest: (request) => {
    40	 *         request.headers.auth = '123';
    41	 *       }
    42	 *     }
    43	 *   }
    44	 *}
    45	 * ```
    46	 *
    47	 *  With httpAgent
    48	 *
    49	 * ```js
    50	 * {
    51	 *   helpers: {
    52	 *     REST: {
    53	 *       endpoint: 'http://site.com/api',
    54	 *       prettyPrintJson: true,
    55	 *       httpAgent: {
    56	 *          key: fs.readFileSync(__dirname + '/path/to/keyfile.key'),
    57	 *          cert: fs.readFileSync(__dirname + '/path/to/certfile.cert'),
    58	 *          rejectUnauthorized: false,
    59	 *          keepAlive: true
    60	 *       }
    61	 *     }
    62	 *   }
    63	 * }
    64	 * ```
    65	 *
    66	 * ```js
    67	 * {
    68	 *   helpers: {
    69	 *     REST: {
    70	 *       endpoint: 'http://site.com/api',
    71	 *       prettyPrintJson: true,
    72	 *       httpAgent: {
    73	 *          ca: fs.readFileSync(__dirname + '/path/to/ca.pem'),
    74	 *          rejectUnauthorized: false,
    75	 *          keepAlive: true
    76	 *       }
    77	 *     }
    78	 *   }
    79	 * }
    80	 * ```
    81	 *
    82	 * ## Access From Helpers
    83	 *
    84	 * Send REST requests by accessing `_executeRequest` method:
    85	 *
    86	 * ```js
    87	 * this.helpers['REST']._executeRequest({
    88	 *    url,
    89	 *    data,
    90	 * });
    91	 * ```
    92	 *
    93	 * ## Methods
    94	 */
    95	class REST extends Helper {
    96	  constructor(config) {
    97	    super(config)
    98	    this.options = {
    99	      timeout: 10000,
   100	      defaultHeaders: {},
   101	      endpoint: '',
   102	      prettyPrintJson: false,
   103	      onRequest: null,
   104	      onResponse: null,
   105	    }
   106	
   107	    if (this.options.maxContentLength) {
   108	      const maxContentLength = this.options.maxUploadFileSize * 1024 * 1024
   109	      this.options.maxContentLength = maxContentLength
   110	      this.options.maxBodyLength = maxContentLength
   111	    }
   112	
   113	    // override defaults with config
   114	    this._setConfig(config)
   115	
   116	    this.headers = { ...this.options.defaultHeaders }
   117	
   118	    // Create an agent with SSL certificate
   119	    if (this.options.httpAgent) {
   120	      // if one of those keys is there, all good to go
   121	      if (this.options.httpAgent.ca || this.options.httpAgent.key || this.options.httpAgent.cert) {
   122	        this.httpsAgent = new Agent(this.options.httpAgent)
   123	      } else {
   124	        // otherwise, throws an error of httpAgent config
   125	        throw Error('Please recheck your httpAgent config!')
   126	      }
   127	    }
   128	
   129	    this.axios = this.httpsAgent ? axios.create({ httpsAgent: this.httpsAgent }) : axios.create()
   130	    // @ts-ignore
   131	    this.axios.defaults.headers = this.options.defaultHeaders
   132	  }
   133	
   134	  static _config() {
   135	    return [
   136	      {
   137	        name: 'endpoint',
   138	        message: 'Endpoint of API you are going to test',
   139	        default: 'http://localhost:3000/api',
   140	      },
   141	    ]
   142	  }
   143	
   144	  static _checkRequirements() {
   145	    try {
   146	      require('axios')
   147	    } catch (e) {
   148	      return ['axios']
   149	    }
   150	  }
   151	
   152	  _before() {
   153	    this.headers = { ...this.options.defaultHeaders }
   154	  }
   155	
   156	  /**
   157	   * Sets request headers for all requests of this test
   158	   *
   159	   * @param {object} headers headers list
   160	   */
   161	  haveRequestHeaders(headers) {
   162	    this.headers = { ...this.headers, ...headers }
   163	  }
   164	
   165	  /**
   166	   * Adds a header for Bearer authentication
   167	   *
   168	   * ```js
   169	   * // we use secret function to hide token from logs
   170	   * I.amBearerAuthenticated(secret('heregoestoken'))
   171	   * ```
   172	   *
   173	   * @param {string | CodeceptJS.Secret} accessToken  Bearer access token
   174	   */
   175	  amBearerAuthenticated(accessToken) {
   176	    this.haveRequestHeaders({ Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}` })
   177	  }
   178	
   179	  /**
   180	   * Executes axios request
   181	   *
   182	   * @param {*} request
   183	   *
   184	   * @returns {Promise<*>} response
   185	   */
   186	  async _executeRequest(request) {
   187	    // Add custom headers. They can be set by amBearerAuthenticated() or haveRequestHeaders()
   188	    request.headers = { ...this.headers, ...request.headers }
   189	
   190	    const _debugRequest = { ...request }
   191	    this.axios.defaults.timeout = request.timeout || this.options.timeout
   192	
   193	    if (this.headers && this.headers.auth) {
   194	      request.auth = this.headers.auth
   195	    }
   196	
   197	    if (typeof request.data === 'object') {
   198	      const returnedValue = {}
   199	      for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(request.data)) {
   200	        returnedValue[key] = value
   201	        if (value instanceof Secret) returnedValue[key] = value.getMasked()
   202	      }
   203	      _debugRequest.data = returnedValue
   204	    }
   205	
   206	    if (request.data instanceof Secret) {
   207	      _debugRequest.data = '*****'
   208	      request.data = typeof request.data === 'object' && !(request.data instanceof Secret) ? { ...request.data.toString() } : request.data.toString()
   209	    }
   210	
   211	    if (typeof request.data === 'string') {
   212	      if (!request.headers || !request.headers['Content-Type']) {
   213	        request.headers = { ...request.headers, ...{ 'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' } }
   214	      }
   215	    }
   216	
   217	    if (this.config.onRequest) {
   218	      await this.config.onRequest(request)
   219	    }
   220	
   221	    this.options.prettyPrintJson ? this.debugSection('Request', beautify(JSON.stringify(_debugRequest))) : this.debugSection('Request', JSON.stringify(_debugRequest))
   222	
   223	    if (this.options.printCurl) {
   224	      this.debugSection('CURL Request', curlize(request))
   225	    }
   226	
   227	    let response
   228	    try {
   229	      response = await this.axios(request)
   230	    } catch (err) {
   231	      if (!err.response) throw err
   232	      this.debugSection('Response', `Response error. Status code: ${err.response.status}`)
   233	      response = err.response
   234	    }
   235	    if (this.config.onResponse) {
   236	      await this.config.onResponse(response)
   237	    }
   238	    this.options.prettyPrintJson ? this.debugSection('Response', beautify(JSON.stringify(response.data))) : this.debugSection('Response', JSON.stringify(response.data))
   239	    return response
   240	  }
   241	
   242	  /**
   243	   * Generates url based on format sent (takes endpoint + url if latter lacks 'http')
   244	   *
   245	   * @param {*} url
   246	   */
   247	  _url(url) {
   248	    return /^\w+\:\/\//.test(url) ? url : this.options.endpoint + url
   249	  }
   250	
   251	  /**
   252	   * Set timeout for the request
   253	   *
   254	   * ```js
   255	   * I.setRequestTimeout(10000); // In milliseconds
   256	   * ```
   257	   *
   258	   * @param {number} newTimeout - timeout in milliseconds
   259	   */
   260	  setRequestTimeout(newTimeout) {
   261	    this.options.timeout = newTimeout
   262	  }
   263	
   264	  /**
   265	   * Send GET request to REST API
   266	   *
   267	   * ```js
   268	   * I.sendGetRequest('/api/users.json');
   269	   * ```
   270	   *
   271	   * @param {*} url
   272	   * @param {object} [headers={}] - the headers object to be sent. By default, it is sent as an empty object
   273	   *
   274	   * @returns {Promise<*>} response
   275	   */
   276	  async sendGetRequest(url, headers = {}) {
   277	    const request = {
   278	      baseURL: this._url(url),
   279	      headers,
   280	    }
   281	    return this._executeRequest(request)
   282	  }
   283	
   284	  /**
   285	   * Sends POST request to API.
   286	   *
   287	   * ```js
   288	   * I.sendPostRequest('/api/users.json', { "email": "user@user.com" });
   289	   *
   290	   * // To mask the payload in logs
   291	   * I.sendPostRequest('/api/users.json', secret({ "email": "user@user.com" }));
   292	   *
   293	   * ```
   294	   *
   295	   * @param {*} url
   296	   * @param {*} [payload={}] - the payload to be sent. By default, it is sent as an empty object
   297	   * @param {object} [headers={}] - the headers object to be sent. By default, it is sent as an empty object
   298	   *
   299	   * @returns {Promise<*>} response
   300	   */
   301	  async sendPostRequest(url, payload = {}, headers = {}) {
   302	    const request = {
   303	      baseURL: this._url(url),
   304	      method: 'POST',
   305	      data: payload,
   306	      headers,
   307	    }
   308	
   309	    if (this.options.maxContentLength) {
   310	      request.maxContentLength = this.options.maxContentLength
   311	      request.maxBodyLength = this.options.maxContentLength
   312	    }
   313	
   314	    return this._executeRequest(request)
   315	  }
   316	
   317	  /**
   318	   * Sends PATCH request to API.
   319	   *
   320	   * ```js
   321	   * I.sendPatchRequest('/api/users.json', { "email": "user@user.com" });
   322	   *
   323	   * // To mask the payload in logs
   324	   * I.sendPatchRequest('/api/users.json', secret({ "email": "user@user.com" }));
   325	   *
   326	   * ```
   327	   *
   328	   * @param {string} url
   329	   * @param {*} [payload={}] - the payload to be sent. By default it is sent as an empty object
   330	   * @param {object} [headers={}] - the headers object to be sent. By default it is sent as an empty object
   331	   *
   332	   * @returns {Promise<*>} response
   333	   */
   334	  async sendPatchRequest(url, payload = {}, headers = {}) {
   335	    const request = {
   336	      baseURL: this._url(url),
   337	      method: 'PATCH',
   338	      data: payload,
   339	      headers,
   340	    }
   341	
   342	    if (this.options.maxContentLength) {
   343	      request.maxContentLength = this.options.maxContentLength
   344	      request.maxBodyLength = this.options.maxBodyLength
   345	    }
   346	
   347	    return this._executeRequest(request)
   348	  }
   349	
   350	  /**
   351	   * Sends PUT request to API.
   352	   *
   353	   * ```js
   354	   * I.sendPutRequest('/api/users.json', { "email": "user@user.com" });
   355	   *
   356	   * // To mask the payload in logs
   357	   * I.sendPutRequest('/api/users.json', secret({ "email": "user@user.com" }));
   358	   *
   359	   * ```
   360	   *
   361	   * @param {string} url
   362	   * @param {*} [payload={}] - the payload to be sent. By default it is sent as an empty object
   363	   * @param {object} [headers={}] - the headers object to be sent. By default it is sent as an empty object
   364	   *
   365	   * @returns {Promise<*>} response
   366	   */
   367	  async sendPutRequest(url, payload = {}, headers = {}) {
   368	    const request = {
   369	      baseURL: this._url(url),
   370	      method: 'PUT',
   371	      data: payload,
   372	      headers,
   373	    }
   374	
   375	    if (this.options.maxContentLength) {
   376	      request.maxContentLength = this.options.maxContentLength
   377	      request.maxBodyLength = this.options.maxBodyLength
   378	    }
   379	
   380	    return this._executeRequest(request)
   381	  }
   382	
   383	  /**
   384	   * Sends DELETE request to API.
   385	   *
   386	   * ```js
   387	   * I.sendDeleteRequest('/api/users/1');
   388	   * ```
   389	   *
   390	   * @param {*} url
   391	   * @param {object} [headers={}] - the headers object to be sent. By default, it is sent as an empty object
   392	   *
   393	   * @returns {Promise<*>} response
   394	   */
   395	  async sendDeleteRequest(url, headers = {}) {
   396	    const request = {
   397	      baseURL: this._url(url),
   398	      method: 'DELETE',
   399	      headers,
   400	    }
   401	
   402	    return this._executeRequest(request)
   403	  }
   404	
   405	  /**
   406	   * Sends DELETE request to API with payload.
   407	   *
   408	   * ```js
   409	   * I.sendDeleteRequestWithPayload('/api/users/1', { author: 'john' });
   410	   * ```
   411	   *
   412	   * @param {*} url
   413	   * @param {*} [payload={}] - the payload to be sent. By default it is sent as an empty object
   414	   * @param {object} [headers={}] - the headers object to be sent. By default, it is sent as an empty object
   415	   *
   416	   * @returns {Promise<*>} response
   417	   */
   418	  async sendDeleteRequestWithPayload(url, payload = {}, headers = {}) {
   419	    const request = {
   420	      baseURL: this._url(url),
   421	      method: 'DELETE',
   422	      data: payload,
   423	      headers,
   424	    }
   425	
   426	    return this._executeRequest(request)
   427	  }
   428	}
   429	
   430	module.exports = REST
   431	
   432	function curlize(request) {
   433	  if (request.data?.constructor.name.toLowerCase() === 'formdata') return 'cURL is not printed as the request body is not a JSON'
   434	  let curl = `curl --location --request ${request.method ? request.method.toUpperCase() : 'GET'} ${request.baseURL} `.replace("'", '')
   435	
   436	  if (request.headers) {
   437	    Object.entries(request.headers).forEach(([key, value]) => {
   438	      curl += `-H "${key}: ${value}" `
   439	    })
   440	  }
   441	
   442	  if (!curl.toLowerCase().includes('content-type: application/json')) {
   443	    curl += '-H "Content-Type: application/json" '
   444	  }
   445	
   446	  if (request.data) {
   447	    curl += `-d '${JSON.stringify(request.data)}'`
   448	  }
   449	  return curl
   450	}
   451

Now I can see the issue. Let me look at the code more carefully. In the constructor, `this.config` is set via the parent class `Helper`. The `_setConfig` method merges config into `this.options`. But in the `_executeRequest` method:

- Line 217-218: `this.config.onRequest` - this checks `this.config` (which comes from the parent Helper class)
- Line 235-236: `this.config.onResponse` - same, checks `this.config`

But in the constructor:
- `this.options.onRequest = null` and `this.options.onResponse = null` are set as defaults
- Then `this._setConfig(config)` is called which should merge the passed config into `this.options`

The issue is that the constructor sets `this.options` as a local variable, but then uses `this.config` to access the callbacks. Let me check how the parent `Helper` class works.

2026-05-30 15:44:44,141 - INFO - swea-agent-codeceptjs__CodeceptJS-5106 - 💭 THOUGHT
Now I understand the issue clearly. Let me trace through the code:

1. In `REST` constructor:
   - `super(config)` is called, which sets `this.config = config`
   - `this.options` is set to a default object with `onRequest: null` and `onResponse: null`
   - Then `this._setConfig(config)` is called, which does `this.options = this._validateConfig(opts)` - this replaces `this.options` entirely with the config passed in.

Wait, let me re-read. `_setConfig` does: `this.options = this._validateConfig(opts);` where `_validateConfig` just returns the config as-is. So `this.options` gets overwritten with the raw config.

But in `_executeRequest`, it uses `this.config.onRequest` and `this.config.onResponse` - NOT `this.options.onRequest` / `this.options.onResponse`.

So the bug is that in `_executeRequest`, the code references `this.config.onRequest` and `this.config.onResponse`, but `this.config` is the original config passed to the constructor (set by `super(config)`). However, `this.options` also has these values after `_setConfig`.

BUT WAIT - the PR description says `onRequest` works but `onResponse` doesn't. Let me look more carefully...

Actually, looking at line 217-218: `if (this.config.onRequest)` - this uses `this.config`
And line 235-236: `if (this.config.onResponse)` - this also uses `this.config`

Both reference `this.config`. So if `onRequest` works, `onResponse` should also work... unless there's something else going on.

Wait, let me look at the `_setConfig` method more carefully. In the parent class:
```js
_setConfig(opts) {
    this.options = this._validateConfig(opts);
}
```

And `_validateConfig` in the parent just returns `config` directly. But in REST's constructor:
```js
this._setConfig(config)
```

This replaces `this.options` with just `config`. But `this.options` was initialized before with defaults including `onRequest: null, onResponse: null`. Now `this.options` is just the passed config.

But the key issue is: `this.config` is set by `super(config)` to the original config. And the `_executeRequest` method checks `this.config.onRequest` and `this.config.onResponse`.

Let me check if there's something else going on. Let me look at the config module to see how helpers are configured

    38	let hooks = [];
    39	let config = {};
    40	
    41	const configFileNames = [
    42	  'codecept.config.js',
    43	  'codecept.conf.js',
    44	  'codecept.js',
    45	  'codecept.config.ts',
    46	  'codecept.conf.ts',
    47	];
    48	
    49	/**
    50	 * Current configuration
    51	 */
    52	class Config {
    53	  /**
    54	   * Create a config with default options
    55	   *
    56	   * @param {*} newConfig
    57	   * @return {Object<string, *>}
    58	   */
    59	  static create(newConfig) {
    60	    config = deepMerge(deepClone(defaultConfig), newConfig);
    61	    hooks.forEach(f => f(config));
    62	    return config;
    63	  }
    64	
    65	  /**
    66	   * Load config from a file.
    67	   * If js file provided: require it and get .config key
    68	   * If json file provided: load and parse JSON
    69	   * If directory provided:
    70	   * * try to load `codecept.config.js` from it
    71	   * * try to load `codecept.conf.js` from it
    72	   * * try to load `codecept.js` from it
    73	   * If none of above: fail.
    74	   *
    75	   * @param {string} configFile
    76	   * @return {*}
    77	   */
    78	  static load(configFile) {
    79	    configFile = path.resolve(configFile || '.');
    80	
    81	    if (!fileExists(configFile)) {
    82	      configFile = configFile.replace('.js', '.ts');
    83	
    84	      if (!fileExists(configFile)) {
    85	        throw new Error(`Config file ${configFile} does not exist. Execute 'codeceptjs init' to create config`);
    86	      }
    87	    }
    88	
    89	    // is config file
    90	    if (isFile(configFile)) {
    91	      return loadConfigFile(configFile);
    92	    }
    93	
    94	    for (const name of configFileNames) {
    95	      // is path to directory
    96	      const jsConfig = path.join(configFile, name);
    97	
    98	      if (isFile(jsConfig)) {
    99	        return loadConfigFile(jsConfig);
   100	      }
   101	    }
   102	
   103	    const configPaths = configFileNames.map(name => path.join(configFile, name)).join(' or ');
   104	
   105	    throw new Error(`Can not load config from ${configPaths}\nCodeceptJS is not initialized in this dir. Execute 'codeceptjs init' to start`);
   106	  }
   107	
   108	  /**
   109	   * Get current config.
   110	   * @param {string} [key]
   111	   * @param {*} [val]
   112	   * @return {*}
   113	   */
   114	  static get(key, val) {
   115	    if (key) {
   116	      return config[key] || val;
   117	    }
   118	    return config;
   119	  }
   120	
   121	  static addHook(fn) {
   122	    hooks.push(fn);
   123	  }
   124	
   125	  /**
   126	   * Appends values to current config
   127	   *
   128	   * @param {Object<string, *>} additionalConfig
   129	   * @return {Object<string, *>}
   130	   */
   131	  static append(additionalConfig) {
   132	    return config = deepMerge(config, additionalConfig);
   133	  }
   134	
   135	  /**
   136	   * Resets config to default
   137	   * @return {Object<string, *>}
   138	   */
   139	  static reset() {
   140	    hooks = [];
   141	    return config = { ...defaultConfig };
   142	  }
   143	}
   144	
   145	module.exports = Config;
   146	
   147	function loadConfigFile(configFile) {
   148	  const extensionName = path.extname(configFile);
   149	
   150	  if (extensionName === '.ts') {
   151	    try {
   152	      require('ts-node/register');
   153	    } catch (err) {
   154	      console.log('ts-node package is required to parse codecept.conf.ts config correctly');
   155	    }
   156	  }
   157	
   158	  // .conf.js config file
   159	  if (extensionName === '.js' || extensionName === '.ts' || extensionName === '.cjs') {
   160	    return Config.create(require(configFile).config);
   161	  }
   162	
   163	  // json config provided
   164	  if (extensionName === '.json') {
   165	    return Config.create(JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(configFile, 'utf8')));
   166	  }
   167	
   168	  throw new Error(`Config file ${configFile} can't be loaded`);
   169	}

    52	  static create(config, opts) {
    53	    debug('creating container')
    54	    asyncHelperPromise = Promise.resolve()
    55	
    56	    // dynamically create mocha instance
    57	    const mochaConfig = config.mocha || {}
    58	    if (config.grep && !opts.grep) mochaConfig.grep = config.grep
    59	    this.createMocha = () => (container.mocha = MochaFactory.create(mochaConfig, opts || {}))
    60	    this.createMocha()
    61	
    62	    // create support objects
    63	    container.support = {}
    64	    container.helpers = createHelpers(config.helpers || {})
    65	    container.translation = loadTranslation(config.translation || null, config.vocabularies || [])
    66	    container.proxySupport = createSupportObjects(config.include || {})
    67	    container.plugins = createPlugins(config.plugins || {}, opts)
    68	    container.result = new Result()
    69	
    70	    createActor(config.include?.I)
    71	
    72	    if (opts && opts.ai) ai.enable(config.ai) // enable AI Assistant
    73	    if (config.gherkin) loadGherkinSteps(config.gherkin.steps || [])
    74	    if (opts && typeof opts.timeouts === 'boolean') store.timeouts = opts.timeouts
    75	  }
    76	
    77	  static actor() {
    78	    return container.support.I
    79	  }
    80	
    81	  /**
    82	   * Get all plugins
    83	   *
    84	   * @api
    85	   * @param {string} [name]
    86	   * @returns { * }
    87	   */
    88	  static plugins(name) {
    89	    if (!name) {
    90	      return container.plugins
    91	    }
    92	    return container.plugins[name]
    93	  }

    95	  /**
    96	   * Get all support objects or get support object by name
    97	   *
    98	   * @api
    99	   * @param {string} [name]

   101	   */
   102	  static support(name) {
   103	    if (!name) {
   104	      return container.proxySupport
   105	    }
   106	    return container.support[name] || container.proxySupport[name]
   107	  }

   116	  static helpers(name) {

   119	    }

   154	  /**
   155	   * Append new services to container
   156	   *
   157	   * @api
   158	   * @param {Object<string, *>} newContainer
   159	   */
   160	  static append(newContainer) {
   161	    const deepMerge = require('./utils').deepMerge
   162	    container = deepMerge(container, newContainer)
   163	    debug('appended', JSON.stringify(newContainer).slice(0, 300))
   164	  }

   166	  /**
   167	   * Clear container
   168	   *
   169	   * @param {Object<string, *>} newHelpers
   170	   * @param {Object<string, *>} newSupport
   171	   * @param {Object<string, *>} newPlugins
   172	   */
   173	  static clear(newHelpers = {}, newSupport = {}, newPlugins = {}) {
   174	    container.helpers = newHelpers
   175	    container.translation = loadTranslation()
   176	    container.proxySupport = createSupportObjects(newSupport)
   177	    container.plugins = newPlugins
   178	    container.sharedKeys = new Set() // Clear shared keys
   179	    asyncHelperPromise = Promise.resolve()
   180	    store.actor = null
   181	    debug('container cleared')
   182	  }

   184	  /**
   185	   * @param {Function|null} fn
   186	   * @returns {Promise<void>}
   187	   */
   188	  static async started(fn = null) {
   189	    if (fn) {
   190	      asyncHelperPromise = asyncHelperPromise.then(fn)
   191	    }
   192	    return asyncHelperPromise
   193	  }
   194	
   195	  /**
   196	   * Share data across worker threads
   197	   *
   198	   * @param {Object} data
   199	   * @param {Object} options - set {local: true} to not share among workers
   200	   */
   201	  static share(data, options = {}) {
   202	    // Instead of using append which replaces the entire container,
   203	    // directly update the support object to maintain proxy references
   204	    Object.assign(container.support, data)
   205	    
   206	    // Track which keys were explicitly shared
   207	    Object.keys(data).forEach(key => container.sharedKeys.add(key))
   208	    
   209	    if (!options.local) {
   210	      WorkerStorage.share(data)
   211	    }
   212	  }

   214	  static createMocha(config = {}, opts = {}) {
   215	    const mochaConfig = config?.mocha || {}
   216	    if (config?.grep && !opts?.grep) {
   217	      mochaConfig.grep = config.grep
   218	    }
   219	    container.mocha = MochaFactory.create(mochaConfig, opts || {})
   220	  }
   221	}

   223	module.exports = Container
   224	
   225	function createHelpers(config) {
   226	  const helpers = {}
   227	  for (let helperName in config) {
   228	    try {
   229	      let HelperClass
   230	
   231	      // ESM import
   232	      if (helperName?.constructor === Function && helperName.prototype) {
   233	        HelperClass = helperName
   234	        helperName = HelperClass.constructor.name
   235	      }
   236	
   237	      // classical require
   238	      if (!HelperClass) {
   239	        HelperClass = requireHelperFromModule(helperName, config)
   240	      }
   241	
   242	      // handle async CJS modules that use dynamic import
   243	      if (isAsyncFunction(HelperClass)) {
   244	        helpers[helperName] = {}
   245	
   246	        asyncHelperPromise = asyncHelperPromise
   247	          .then(() => HelperClass())
   248	          .then(ResolvedHelperClass => {
   249	            // Check if ResolvedHelperClass is a constructor function
   250	            if (typeof ResolvedHelperClass?.constructor !== 'function') {
   251	              throw new Error(`Helper class from module '${helperName}' is not a class. Use CJS async module syntax.`)
   252	            }
   253	
   254	            debug(`helper ${helperName} async initialized`)
   255	
   256	            helpers[helperName] = new ResolvedHelperClass(config[helperName])
   257	          })
   258	
   259	        continue
   260	      }
   261	
   262	      checkHelperRequirements(HelperClass)
   263	
   264	      helpers[helperName] = new HelperClass(config[helperName])
   265	      debug(`helper ${helperName} initialized`)
   266	    } catch (err) {
   267	      throw new Error(`Could not load helper ${helperName} (${err.message})`)
   268	    }
   269	  }
   270	
   271	  for (const name in helpers) {
   272	    if (helpers[name]._init) helpers[name]._init()
   273	  }
   274	  return helpers
   275	}
   276	
   277	function checkHelperRequirements(HelperClass) {
   278	  if (HelperClass._checkRequirements) {
   279	    const requirements = HelperClass._checkRequirements()
   280	    if (requirements) {
   281	      let install
   282	      if (installedLocally()) {
   283	        install = `npm install --save-dev ${requirements.join(' ')}`
   284	      } else {
   285	        console.log('WARNING: CodeceptJS is not installed locally. It is recommended to switch to local installation')
   286	        install = `[sudo] npm install -g ${requirements.join(' ')}`
   287	      }
   288	      throw new Error(`Required modules are not installed.\n\nRUN: ${install}`)
   289	    }
   290	  }
   291	}
   292	
   293	function requireHelperFromModule(helperName, config, HelperClass) {
   294	  const moduleName = getHelperModuleName(helperName, config)
   295	  if (moduleName.startsWith('./helper/')) {
   296	    HelperClass = require(moduleName)
   297	  } else {
   298	    // check if the new syntax export default HelperName is used and loads the Helper, otherwise loads the module that used old syntax export = HelperName.
   299	    try {
   300	      const mod = require(moduleName)
   301	      if (!mod && !mod.default) {
   302	        throw new Error(`Helper module '${moduleName}' was not found. Make sure you have installed the package correctly.`)
   303	      }
   304	      HelperClass = mod.default || mod
   305	    } catch (err) {
   306	      if (err.code === 'MODULE_NOT_FOUND') {
   307	        throw new Error(`Helper module '${moduleName}' was not found. Make sure you have installed the package correctly.`)
   308	      }
   309	      throw err
   310	    }
   311	  }
   312	  return HelperClass
   313	}
   314	
   315	function createSupportObjects(config) {
   316	  const asyncWrapper = function (f) {
   317	    return function () {
   318	      return f.apply(this, arguments).catch(e => {
   319	        recorder.saveFirstAsyncError(e)
   320	        throw e
   321	      })
   322	    }
   323	  }
   324	
   325	  function lazyLoad(name) {
   326	    return new Proxy(
   327	      {},
   328	      {
   329	        get(target, prop) {
   330	          // behavr like array or
   331	          if (prop === 'length') return Object.keys(config).length
   332	          if (prop === Symbol.iterator) {
   333	            return function* () {
   334	              for (let i = 0; i < Object.keys(config).length; i++) {
   335	                yield target[i]
   336	              }
   337	            }
   338	          }
   339	
   340	          // load actual name from vocabulary
   341	          if (container.translation.name) {
   342	            name = container.translation.name
   343	          }
   344	
   345	          if (name === 'I') {
   346	            const actor = createActor(config.I)
   347	            methodsOfObject(actor)
   348	            return actor[prop]
   349	          }
   350	
   351	          if (!container.support[name] && typeof config[name] === 'object') {
   352	            container.support[name] = config[name]
   353	          }
   354	
   355	          if (!container.support[name]) {
   356	            // Load object on first access
   357	            const supportObject = loadSupportObject(config[name])
   358	            container.support[name] = supportObject
   359	            try {
   360	              if (container.support[name]._init) {
   361	                container.support[name]._init()
   362	              }
   363	              debug(`support object ${name} initialized`)
   364	            } catch (err) {
   365	              throw new Error(`Initialization failed for ${name}: ${container.support[name]}\n${err.message}\n${err.stack}`)
   366	            }
   367	          }
   368	
   369	          const currentObject = container.support[name]
   370	          let currentValue = currentObject[prop]
   371	
   372	          if (isFunction(currentValue) || isAsyncFunction(currentValue)) {
   373	            const ms = new MetaStep(name, prop)
   374	            ms.setContext(currentObject)
   375	            if (isAsyncFunction(currentValue)) currentValue = asyncWrapper(currentValue)
   376	            debug(`metastep is created for ${name}.${prop.toString()}()`)
   377	            return ms.run.bind(ms, currentValue)
   378	          }
   379	
   380	          return currentValue
   381	        },
   382	        has(target, prop) {
   383	          container.support[name] = container.support[name] || loadSupportObject(config[name])
   384	          return prop in container.support[name]
   385	        },
   386	        getOwnPropertyDescriptor(target, prop) {
   387	          container.support[name] = container.support[name] || loadSupportObject(config[name])
   388	          return {
   389	            enumerable: true,
   390	            configurable: true,
   391	            value: this.get(target, prop),
   392	          }
   393	        },
   394	        ownKeys() {
   395	          container.support[name] = container.support[name] || loadSupportObject(config[name])
   396	          return Reflect.ownKeys(container.support[name])
   397	        },
   398	      },
   399	    )
   400	  }
   401	
   402	  const keys = Reflect.ownKeys(config)
   403	  return new Proxy(
   404	    {},
   405	    {
   406	      has(target, key) {
   407	        return keys.includes(key) || container.sharedKeys.has(key)
   408	      },
   409	      ownKeys() {
   410	        // Return both original config keys and explicitly shared keys
   411	        return [...new Set([...keys, ...container.sharedKeys])]
   412	      },
   413	      getOwnPropertyDescriptor(target, prop) {
   414	        return {
   415	          enumerable: true,
   416	          configurable: true,
   417	          value: this.get(target, prop),
   418	        }
   419	      },
   420	      get(target, key) {
   421	        // First check if this is an explicitly shared property
   422	        if (container.sharedKeys.has(key) && key in container.support) {
   423	          return container.support[key]
   424	        }
   425	        return lazyLoad(key)
   426	      },
   427	    },
   428	  )
   429	}
   430	
   431	function createActor(actorPath) {
   432	  if (container.support.I) return container.support.I
   433	
   434	  if (actorPath) {
   435	    container.support.I = loadSupportObject(actorPath)
   436	  } else {
   437	    const actor = require('./actor')
   438	    container.support.I = actor()
   439	  }
   440	
   441	  return container.support.I
   442	}
   443	
   444	function createPlugins(config, options = {}) {
   445	  const plugins = {}
   446	
   447	  const enabledPluginsByOptions = (options.plugins || '').split(',')
   448	  for (const pluginName in config) {
   449	    if (!config[pluginName]) config[pluginName] = {}
   450	    if (!config[pluginName].enabled && enabledPluginsByOptions.indexOf(pluginName) < 0) {
   451	      continue // plugin is disabled
   452	    }
   453	    let module
   454	    try {
   455	      if (config[pluginName].require) {
   456	        module = config[pluginName].require
   457	        if (module.startsWith('.')) {
   458	          // local
   459	          module = path.resolve(global.codecept_dir, module) // custom plugin
   460	        }
   461	      } else {
   462	        module = `./plugin/${pluginName}`
   463	      }
   464	      plugins[pluginName] = require(module)(config[pluginName])
   465	    } catch (err) {
   466	      throw new Error(`Could not load plugin ${pluginName} from module '${module}':\n${err.message}\n${err.stack}`)
   467	    }
   468	  }
   469	  return plugins
   470	}
   471	
   472	function loadGherkinSteps(paths) {
   473	  global.Before = fn => event.dispatcher.on(event.test.started, fn)
   474	  global.After = fn => event.dispatcher.on(event.test.finished, fn)
   475	  global.Fail = fn => event.dispatcher.on(event.test.failed, fn)
   476	
   477	  // If gherkin.steps is string, then this will iterate through that folder and send all step def js files to loadSupportObject
   478	  // If gherkin.steps is Array, it will go the old way
   479	  // This is done so that we need not enter all Step Definition files under config.gherkin.steps
   480	  if (Array.isArray(paths)) {
   481	    for (const path of paths) {
   482	      loadSupportObject(path, `Step Definition from ${path}`)
   483	    }
   484	  } else {
   485	    const folderPath = paths.startsWith('.') ? normalizeAndJoin(global.codecept_dir, paths) : ''
   486	    if (folderPath !== '') {
   487	      globSync(folderPath).forEach(file => {
   488	        loadSupportObject(file, `Step Definition from ${file}`)
   489	      })
   490	    }
   491	  }
   492	
   493	  delete global.Before
   494	  delete global.After
   495	  delete global.Fail
   496	}
   497	
   498	function loadSupportObject(modulePath, supportObjectName) {
   499	  if (!modulePath) {
   500	    throw new Error(`Support object "${supportObjectName}" is not defined`)
   501	  }
   502	  if (modulePath.charAt(0) === '.') {
   503	    modulePath = path.join(global.codecept_dir, modulePath)
   504	  }
   505	  try {
   506	    const obj = require(modulePath)
   507	
   508	    // Handle different types of imports
   509	    if (typeof obj === 'function') {
   510	      // If it's a class (constructor function)
   511	      if (obj.prototype && obj.prototype.constructor === obj) {
   512	        const ClassName = obj
   513	        return new ClassName()
   514	      }
   515	      // If it's a regular function
   516	      return obj()
   517	    }
   518	
   519	    if (obj && Array.isArray(obj)) {
   520	      return obj
   521	    }
   522	
   523	    // If it's a plain object
   524	    if (obj && typeof obj === 'object') {
   525	      return obj
   526	    }
   527	
   528	    throw new Error(`Support object "${supportObjectName}" should be an object, class, or function, but got ${typeof obj}`)
   529	  } catch (err) {
   530	    throw new Error(`Could not include object ${supportObjectName} from module '${modulePath}'\n${err.message}\n${err.stack}`)
   531	  }
   532	}
   533	
   534	/**
   535	 * Method collect own property and prototype
   536	 */
   537	
   538	function loadTranslation(locale, vocabularies) {
   539	  if (!locale) {
   540	    return Translation.createEmpty()
   541	  }
   542	
   543	  let translation
   544	
   545	  // check if it is a known translation
   546	  if (Translation.langs[locale]) {
   547	    translation = new Translation(Translation.langs[locale])
   548	  } else if (fileExists(path.join(global.codecept_dir, locale))) {
   549	    // get from a provided file instead
   550	    translation = Translation.createDefault()
   551	    translation.loadVocabulary(locale)
   552	  } else {
   553	    translation = Translation.createDefault()
   554	  }
   555	
   556	  vocabularies.forEach(v => translation.loadVoc<response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you.     }
```js
    // ... 
    this._setConfig(config)  // this.options = this._validateConfig(opts) = config
```

Wait, `_setConfig` replaces `this.options` entirely with the config. So `this.options` becomes the config object. But `this.config` also is the config object (set by `super(config)`).

So both `this.config.onRequest` and `this.config.onResponse` should work... unless there's something else.

Wait, I need to re-read. The `_setConfig` method does:
```js
_setConfig(opts) {
    this.options = this._validateConfig(opts);
}
```

And `_validateConfig` returns `config` directly. So `this.options` = the passed config.

But in the constructor:
```js
this.options = {
    timeout: 10000,
    ...
    onRequest: null,
    onResponse: null,
}
// ...
this._setConfig(config)
```

So after `_setConfig`, `this.options` is overwritten with `config`. But then later in `_executeRequest`, the code uses `this.options.timeout`, `this.options.prettyPrintJson`, `this.options.printCurl`, etc. So `this.options` is used for some things and `this.config` for others.

But wait, `this.config` should also be the same config object... Let me double-check. In the parent constructor:
```js
constructor(config) {
    this.config = config;
}
```

So `this.config = config` and then `this._setConfig(config)` sets `this.options = config`. So both point to the same object. That should work...

Unless the problem is that `onResponse` is called but there's some issue with the response object not having the `request` property. Let me think again...

Actually, I think the issue might be different. Let me look at the `_executeRequest` method more carefully:

```js
try {
    response = await this.axios(request)
} catch (err) {
    if (!err.response) throw err
    this.debugSection('Response', `Response error. Status code: ${err.response.status}`)
    response = err.response
}
if (this.config.onResponse) {
    await this.config.onResponse(response)
}
```

When there's an error, it catches and uses `err.response`. But in the normal case, `response = await this.axios(request)`. 

With axios, the response object has a `request` property that refers to the request. But wait - the `onRequest` callback receives the `request` object directly and modifies it. The `onResponse` callback receives the `response` object. The PR description says:

```ts
onResponse: (response) => {
    console.log('onResponse');
    const startTime = response.request.headers[ 'start-time' ];
    const endTime = new Date().getTime();
    const responseTime = endTime - startTime;
    response.Headers[ 'x-response-time' ] = responseTime;
},
```

They expect `response.request` to be the original request with their custom header. But maybe `onResponse` is simply not being called at all?

Let me actually create a test script to reproduce this.

But `this.config` remains the original config passed to `super(config)`.\n\nNow here\'s the thing: the test at line 119-124 does `I.config.onRequest = ...`. But in the user\'s scenario, they set `onRequest` and `onResponse` in the config file. The config is passed to the constructor. `this.config` should have both `onRequest` and `onResponse`.\n\nBut wait - there\'s a subtlety. Let me look at how `createHelpers` works:\n\n```js\nhelpers[helperName] = new HelperClass(config[helperName])\n```\n\nSo `config[helperName]` is the helper-specific config, e.g., `{ endpoint: \'...\', onRequest: ..., onResponse: ... }`. This is passed to the constructor.\n\nIn the constructor:\n1. `super(config)` sets `this.config = config`\n2. `this.options = { ..., onRequest: null, onResponse: null }`\n3. `this._setConfig(config)` sets `this.options = config` (overwrites the defaults)\n\nSo after construction:\n- `this.config` = the original config object (with `onRequest`, `onResponse`, `endpoint`, etc.)\n- `this.options` = the same config object\n\nBoth should have `onRequest` and `onResponse`. Interesting! Look at line 119-124:

```js

    I.config.onRequest = request => (request.data = { name: 'Vasya' })

    response.data.name.should.eql('Vasya')

```

So the test directly sets `I.config.onRequest`. That confirms that `this.config` is used. But there's NO test for `onResponse`. And the PR description says `onRequest` works but `onResponse` doesn't.

Wait, let me re-analyze. If `onRequest` works (as the PR author says), then `this.config.onRequest` is being called. And `this.config.onResponse` should also be called since they're both accessed the same way...

Unless the issue is something else entirely. Let me look at the axios response object. In axios, the response object has a `config` property (not `request`). But the user is accessing `response.request.headers`. However, the PR says they don't see "onResponse" being logged, meaning the callback isn't called at all.

Let me create a quick reproduction script.

Actually wait, let me check if there's a `_setConfig` override in REST that might be causing the issue. Looking at the constructor again:

```js
constructor(config) {
    super(config)   // sets this.config = config
    this.options = {
        timeout: 10000,
        defaultHeaders: {},
        endpoint: '',
        prettyPrintJson: false,
        onRequest: null,
        onResponse: null,
    }
    // ...
    this._setConfig(config)
```

`_setConfig` comes from the parent:
```js
_setConfig(opts) {
    this.options = this._validateConfig(opts);
}
```

And `_validateConfig`:
```js
_validateConfig(config) {
    return config;
}
```

So `this.options` gets replaced by the raw config. But `this.config` remains the original config passed to `super(config)`.

Now here's the thing: the test at line 119-124 does `I.config.onRequest = ...`. But in the user's scenario, they set `onRequest` and `onResponse` in the config file. The config is passed to the constructor. `this.config` should have both `onRequest` and `onResponse`.

But wait - there's a subtlety. Let me check if maybe the config is being processed differently. Let me look at how `createHelpers` works:

```js
helpers[helperName] = new HelperClass(config[helperName])
```

So `config[helperName]` is the helper-specific config, e.g., `{ endpoint: '...', onRequest: ..., onResponse: ... }`. This is passed to the constructor.

In the constructor:
1. `super(config)` sets `this.config = config`
2. `this.options = { ..., onRequest: null, onResponse: null }`
3. `this._setConfig(config)` sets `this.options = config` (overwrites the defaults)

So after construction:
- `this.config` = the original config object (with `onRequest`, `onResponse`, `endpoint`, etc.)
- `this.options` = the same config object

Both should have `onRequest` and `onResponse`. So `this.config.onRequest` and `this.config.onResponse` should work...

But wait, there's another possibility. Let me look at the `_executeRequest` more carefully:

```js
if (this.config.onRequest) {
    await this.config.onRequest(request)
}
```

What if `this.config.onRequest` is a function, so it's truthy, but `this.config.onResponse` is also a function? They should both be called.

     1	const Helper = require('@codeceptjs/helper')
     2	const assert = require('assert')
     3	const joi = require('joi')
     4	
     5	/**
     6	 * This helper allows performing assertions on JSON responses paired with following helpers:
     7	 *
     8	 * * REST
     9	 * * GraphQL
    10	 * * Playwright
    11	 *
    12	 * It can check status codes, response data, response structure.
    13	 *
    14	 *
    15	 * ## Configuration
    16	 *
    17	 * * `requestHelper` - a helper which will perform requests. `REST` by default, also `Playwright` or `GraphQL` can be used. Custom helpers must have `onResponse` hook in their config, which will be executed when request is performed.
    18	 *
    19	 * ### Examples
    20	 *
    21	 * Zero-configuration when paired with REST:
    22	 *
    23	 * ```js
    24	 * // inside codecept.conf.js
    25	 *{
    26	 *   helpers: {
    27	 *     REST: {
    28	 *       endpoint: 'http://site.com/api',
    29	 *     },
    30	 *     JSONResponse: {}
    31	 *   }
    32	 *}
    33	 * ```
    34	 * Explicitly setting request helper if you use `makeApiRequest` of Playwright to perform requests and not paired REST:
    35	 *
    36	 * ```js
    37	 * // inside codecept.conf.js
    38	 * // ...
    39	 *   helpers: {
    40	 *     Playwright: {
    41	 *       url: 'https://localhost',
    42	 *       browser: 'chromium',
    43	 *     },
    44	 *     JSONResponse: {
    45	 *       requestHelper: 'Playwright',
    46	 *     }
    47	 *   }
    48	 * ```
    49	 * ## Access From Helpers
    50	 *
    51	 * If you plan to add custom assertions it is recommended to create a helper that will retrieve response object from JSONResponse:
    52	 *
    53	 *
    54	 * ```js
    55	 * // inside custom helper
    56	 * const response = this.helpers.JSONResponse.response;
    57	 * ```
    58	 *
    59	 * ## Methods
    60	 */
    61	class JSONResponse extends Helper {
    62	  constructor(config = {}) {
    63	    super(config)
    64	    this.options = {
    65	      requestHelper: 'REST',
    66	    }
    67	    this.options = { ...this.options, ...config }
    68	  }
    69	
    70	  _beforeSuite() {
    71	    this.response = null
    72	    if (!this.helpers[this.options.requestHelper]) {
    73	      throw new Error(`Error setting JSONResponse, helper ${this.options.requestHelper} is not enabled in config, helpers: ${Object.keys(this.helpers)}`)
    74	    }
    75	    // connect to REST helper
    76	    this.helpers[this.options.requestHelper].config.onResponse = response => {
    77	      this.response = response
    78	    }
    79	  }
    80	
    81	  _before() {
    82	    this.response = null
    83	  }
    84	
    85	  static _checkRequirements() {
    86	    try {
    87	      require('joi')
    88	    } catch (e) {
    89	      return ['joi']
    90	    }
    91	  }
    92	
    93	  /**
    94	   * Checks that response code is equal to the provided one
    95	   *
    96	   * ```js
    97	   * I.seeResponseCodeIs(200);
    98	   * ```
    99	   *
   100	   * @param {number} code
   101	   */
   102	  seeResponseCodeIs(code) {
   103	    this._checkResponseReady()
   104	    assert.strictEqual(this.response.status, code, 'Response code is not the same as expected')
   105	  }
   106	
   107	  /**
   108	   * Checks that response code is not equal to the provided one
   109	   *
   110	   * ```js
   111	   * I.dontSeeResponseCodeIs(500);
   112	   * ```
   113	   *
   114	   * @param {number} code
   115	   */
   116	  dontSeeResponseCodeIs(code) {
   117	    this._checkResponseReady()
   118	    assert.notStrictEqual(this.response.status, code)
   119	  }
   120	
   121	  /**
   122	   * Checks that the response code is 4xx
   123	   */
   124	  seeResponseCodeIsClientError() {
   125	    this._checkResponseReady()
   126	    assert(this.response.status >= 400 && this.response.status < 500)
   127	  }
   128	
   129	  /**
   130	   * Checks that the response code is 3xx
   131	   */
   132	  seeResponseCodeIsRedirection() {
   133	    this._checkResponseReady()
   134	    assert(this.response.status >= 300 && this.response.status < 400)
   135	  }
   136	
   137	  /**
   138	   * Checks that the response code is 5xx
   139	   */
   140	  seeResponseCodeIsServerError() {
   141	    this._checkResponseReady()
   142	    assert(this.response.status >= 500 && this.response.status < 600)
   143	  }
   144	
   145	  /**
   146	   * Checks that the response code is 2xx
   147	   * Use it instead of seeResponseCodeIs(200) if server can return 204 instead.
   148	   *
   149	   * ```js
   150	   * I.seeResponseCodeIsSuccessful();
   151	   * ```
   152	   */
   153	  seeResponseCodeIsSuccessful() {
   154	    this._checkResponseReady()
   155	    assert(this.response.status >= 200 && this.response.status < 300)
   156	  }
   157	
   158	  /**
   159	   * Checks for deep inclusion of a provided json in a response data.
   160	   *
   161	   * ```js
   162	   * // response.data == { user: { name: 'jon', email: 'jon@doe.com' } }
   163	   *
   164	   * I.seeResponseContainsJson({ user: { email: 'jon@doe.com' } });
   165	   * ```
   166	   * If an array is received, checks that at least one element contains JSON
   167	   * ```js
   168	   * // response.data == [{ user: { name: 'jon', email: 'jon@doe.com' } }]
   169	   *
   170	   * I.seeResponseContainsJson({ user: { email: 'jon@doe.com' } });
   171	   * ```
   172	   *
   173	   * @param {object} json
   174	   */
   175	  seeResponseContainsJson(json = {}) {
   176	    this._checkResponseReady()
   177	    if (Array.isArray(this.response.data)) {
   178	      let found = false
   179	      for (const el of this.response.data) {
   180	        try {
   181	          this._assertContains(el, json)
   182	          found = true
   183	          break
   184	        } catch (err) {
   185	          continue
   186	        }
   187	      }
   188	      assert(found, `No elements in array matched ${JSON.stringify(json)}`)
   189	    } else {
   190	      this._assertContains(this.response.data, json)
   191	    }
   192	  }
   193	
   194	  /**
   195	   * Checks for deep inclusion of a provided json in a response data.
   196	   *
   197	   * ```js
   198	   * // response.data == { data: { user: 1 } }
   199	   *
   200	   * I.dontSeeResponseContainsJson({ user: 2 });
   201	   * ```
   202	   * If an array is received, checks that no element of array contains json:
   203	   * ```js
   204	   * // response.data == [{ user: 1 }, { user: 3 }]
   205	   *
   206	   * I.dontSeeResponseContainsJson({ user: 2 });
   207	   * ```
   208	   *
   209	   * @param {object} json
   210	   */
   211	  dontSeeResponseContainsJson(json = {}) {
   212	    this._checkResponseReady()
   213	    if (Array.isArray(this.response.data)) {
   214	      for (const data of this.response.data) {
   215	        try {
   216	          this._assertContains(data, json)
   217	          assert.fail(`Found matching element: ${JSON.stringify(data)}`)
   218	        } catch (err) {
   219	          // expected to fail
   220	          continue
   221	        }
   222	      }
   223	    } else {
   224	      try {
   225	        this._assertContains(this.response.data, json)
   226	        assert.fail('Response contains the JSON')
   227	      } catch (err) {
   228	        // expected to fail
   229	      }
   230	    }
   231	  }
   232	
   233	  /**
   234	   * Checks for deep inclusion of a provided json in a response data.
   235	   *
   236	   * ```js
   237	   * // response.data == { user: { name: 'jon', email: 'jon@doe.com' } }
   238	   *
   239	   * I.seeResponseContainsKeys(['user']);
   240	   * ```
   241	   *
   242	   * If an array is received, check is performed for each element of array:
   243	   *
   244	   * ```js
   245	   * // response.data == [{ user: 'jon' }, { user: 'matt'}]
   246	   *
   247	   * I.seeResponseContainsKeys(['user']);
   248	   * ```
   249	   *
   250	   * @param {array} keys
   251	   */
   252	  seeResponseContainsKeys(keys = []) {
   253	    this._checkResponseReady()
   254	    if (Array.isArray(this.response.data)) {
   255	      for (const data of this.response.data) {
   256	        for (const key of keys) {
   257	          assert(key in data, `Key "${key}" is not found in ${JSON.stringify(data)}`)
   258	        }
   259	      }
   260	    } else {
   261	      for (const key of keys) {
   262	        assert(key in this.response.data, `Key "${key}" is not found in ${JSON.stringify(this.response.data)}`)
   263	      }
   264	    }
   265	  }
   266	
   267	  /**
   268	   * Executes a callback function passing in `response` object and assert
   269	   * Use it to perform custom checks of response data
   270	   *
   271	   * ```js
   272	   * I.seeResponseValidByCallback(({ data, status }) => {
   273	   *   assert.strictEqual(status, 200);
   274	   *   assert('user' in data);
   275	   *   assert('company' in data);
   276	   * });
   277	   * ```
   278	   *
   279	   * @param {function} fn
   280	   */
   281	  seeResponseValidByCallback(fn) {
   282	    this._checkResponseReady()
   283	    fn({ ...this.response, assert })
   284	    const body = fn.toString()
   285	    fn.toString = () => `${body.split('\n')[1]}...`
   286	  }
   287	
   288	  /**
   289	   * Checks that response data equals to expected:
   290	   *
   291	   * ```js
   292	   * // response.data is { error: 'Not allowed' }
   293	   *
   294	   * I.seeResponseEquals({ error: 'Not allowed' })
   295	   * ```
   296	   * @param {object} resp
   297	   */
   298	  seeResponseEquals(resp) {
   299	    this._checkResponseReady()
   300	    assert.deepStrictEqual(this.response.data, resp)
   301	  }
   302	
   303	  /**
   304	   * Validates JSON structure of response using [joi library](https://joi.dev).
   305	   * See [joi API](https://joi.dev/api/) for complete reference on usage.
   306	   *
   307	   * Use pre-initialized joi instance by passing function callback:
   308	   *
   309	   * ```js
   310	   * // response.data is { name: 'jon', id: 1 }
   311	   *
   312	   * I.seeResponseMatchesJsonSchema(joi => {
   313	   *   return joi.object({
   314	   *     name: joi.string(),
   315	   *     id: joi.number()
   316	   *   })
   317	   * });
   318	   *
   319	   * // or pass a valid schema
   320	   * const joi = require('joi');
   321	   *
   322	   * I.seeResponseMatchesJsonSchema(joi.object({
   323	   *   name: joi.string(),
   324	   *   id: joi.number();
   325	   * });
   326	   * ```
   327	   *
   328	   * @param {any} fnOrSchema
   329	   */
   330	  seeResponseMatchesJsonSchema(fnOrSchema) {
   331	    this._checkResponseReady()
   332	    let schema = fnOrSchema
   333	    if (typeof fnOrSchema === 'function') {
   334	      schema = fnOrSchema(joi)
   335	      const body = fnOrSchema.toString()
   336	      fnOrSchema.toString = () => `${body.split('\n')[1]}...`
   337	    }
   338	    if (!schema) throw new Error('Empty Joi schema provided, see https://joi.dev/ for details')
   339	    if (!joi.isSchema(schema)) throw new Error('Invalid Joi schema provided, see https://joi.dev/ for details')
   340	    schema.toString = () => schema.describe()
   341	    joi.assert(this.response.data, schema)
   342	  }
   343	
   344	  _checkResponseReady() {
   345	    if (!this.response) throw new Error('Response is not available')
   346	  }
   347	
   348	  _assertContains(actual, expected) {
   349	    for (const key in expected) {
   350	      assert(key in actual, `Key "${key}" not found in ${JSON.stringify(actual)}`)
   351	      if (typeof expected[key] === 'object' && expected[key] !== null) {
   352	        if (Array.isArray(expected[key])) {
   353	          // Handle array comparison: each expected element should have a match in actual array
   354	          assert(Array.isArray(actual[key]), `Expected array for key "${key}", but got ${typeof actual[key]}`)
   355	          for (const expectedItem of expected[key]) {
   356	            let found = false
   357	            for (const actualItem of actual[key]) {
   358	              try {
   359	                this._assertContains(actualItem, expectedItem)
   360	                found = true
   361	                break
   362	              } catch (err) {
   363	                continue
   364	              }
   365	            }
   366	            assert(found, `No matching element found in array for ${JSON.stringify(expectedItem)}`)
   367	          }
   368	        } else {
   369	          this._assertContains(actual[key], expected[key])
   370	        }
   371	      } else {
   372	        assert.deepStrictEqual(actual[key], expected[key], `Values for key "${key}" don't match`)
   373	      }
   374	    }
   375	  }
   376	}
   377	
   378	module.exports = JSONResponse
   379

2026-05-30 15:45:38,715 - INFO - swea-agent-codeceptjs__CodeceptJS-5106 - 💭 THOUGHT
Now I found the issue! Look at line 76-78 in `JSONResponse.js`:

```js
this.helpers[this.options.requestHelper].config.onResponse = response => {
    this.response = response
}
```

The `JSONResponse` helper **overwrites** `this.helpers['REST'].config.onResponse` with its own callback! So if a user has both REST's `onResponse` config AND JSONResponse, the user's `onResponse` gets replaced by JSONResponse's internal callback.

But wait, the PR description says the user's `onResponse` doesn't work. They have both `REST` and `JSONResponse` helpers configured. The `JSONResponse` helper's `_beforeSuite` overwrites the REST helper's `config.onResponse`. So:

1. User configures `onResponse` in REST config
2. JSONResponse's `_beforeSuite` is called during initialization
3. JSONResponse replaces `REST.config.onResponse` with its own function
4. User's `onResponse` is never called

But the user still sees `onRequest` working because JSONResponse doesn't touch `onRequest`.

Let me verify this theory by looking at the initialization order. Let me check if `_beforeSuite` is called during container creation.

Looking at `createHelpers`:
```js
function createHelpers(config) {
    const helpers = {}
    for (let helperName in config) {
        // ...
        helpers[helperName] = new HelperClass(config[helperName])
    }
    for (const name in helpers) {
        if (helpers[name]._init) helpers[name]._init()
    }
    return helpers
}
```

And then in `Container.create`:
```js
static create(config, opts) {
    // ...
    container.helpers = createHelpers(config.helpers || {})
    // ...
}
```

But `_beforeSuite` is not called during creation. Let me check where `_beforeSuite` is called...

     1	const path = require('path')
     2	const event = require('../event')
     3	const container = require('../container')
     4	const recorder = require('../recorder')
     5	const store = require('../store')
     6	const { error } = require('../output')
     7	/**
     8	 * Enable Helpers to listen to test events
     9	 */
    10	module.exports = function () {
    11	  const helpers = container.helpers()
    12	
    13	  const runHelpersHook = (hook, param) => {
    14	    if (store.dryRun) return
    15	    Object.values(helpers).forEach(helper => {
    16	      if (helper[hook]) {
    17	        helper[hook](param)
    18	      }
    19	    })
    20	  }
    21	
    22	  const runAsyncHelpersHook = (hook, param, force) => {
    23	    if (store.dryRun) return
    24	    Object.keys(helpers).forEach(key => {
    25	      if (!helpers[key][hook]) return
    26	      recorder.add(`hook ${key}.${hook}()`, () => helpers[key][hook](param), force, false)
    27	    })
    28	  }
    29	
    30	  event.dispatcher.on(event.suite.before, suite => {
    31	    // if (suite.parent) return; // only for root suite
    32	    runAsyncHelpersHook('_beforeSuite', suite, true)
    33	  })
    34	
    35	  event.dispatcher.on(event.suite.after, suite => {
    36	    // if (suite.parent) return; // only for root suite
    37	    runAsyncHelpersHook('_afterSuite', suite, true)
    38	  })
    39	
    40	  event.dispatcher.on(event.test.started, test => {
    41	    runHelpersHook('_test', test)
    42	    recorder.catch(e => error(e))
    43	  })
    44	
    45	  event.dispatcher.on(event.test.before, test => {
    46	    // schedule config to revert changes
    47	    runAsyncHelpersHook('_before', test, true)
    48	    recorder.catchWithoutStop(e => error(e))
    49	  })
    50	
    51	  event.dispatcher.on(event.test.passed, test => {
    52	    runAsyncHelpersHook('_passed', test, true)
    53	    // should not fail test execution, so errors should be caught
    54	    recorder.catchWithoutStop(e => error(e))
    55	  })
    56	
    57	  event.dispatcher.on(event.test.failed, test => {
    58	    runAsyncHelpersHook('_failed', test, true)
    59	    // should not fail test execution, so errors should be caught
    60	    recorder.catchWithoutStop(e => error(e))
    61	  })
    62	
    63	  event.dispatcher.on(event.test.after, () => {
    64	    runAsyncHelpersHook('_after', {}, true)
    65	    recorder.catchWithoutStop(e => error(e))
    66	  })
    67	
    68	  event.dispatcher.on(event.step.before, step => {
    69	    runAsyncHelpersHook('_beforeStep', step)
    70	  })
    71	
    72	  event.dispatcher.on(event.step.after, step => {
    73	    runAsyncHelpersHook('_afterStep', step)
    74	  })
    75	
    76	  event.dispatcher.on(event.all.result, () => {
    77	    runAsyncHelpersHook('_finishTest', {}, true)
    78	  })
    79	}

So the flow is:
1. Container creates helpers
2. Test suite starts → `_beforeSuite` is called on all helpers
3. JSONResponse's `_beforeSuite` overwrites REST's `config.onResponse`

Now I understand the issue! The JSONResponse helper replaces the user's `onResponse` callback. This is the bug.

But wait, the PR description says the user uses `JSONResponse: {}`. But they also have `onResponse` in their REST config. The JSONResponse helper's `_beforeSuite` blindly overwrites `onResponse`:

```js
this.helpers[this.options.requestHelper].config.onResponse = response => {
    this.response = response
}
```

This doesn't preserve the original `onResponse` if one was set. The fix should be to save the original `onResponse` and call it from within the new `onResponse`, or to use a different mechanism entirely (like using an event or a different property).

const Helper = require('@codeceptjs/helper')
const assert = require('assert')
const joi = require('joi')

/**
 * This helper allows performing assertions on JSON responses paired with following helpers:
 *
 * * REST
 * * GraphQL
 * * Playwright
 *
 * It can check status codes, response data, response structure.
 *
 *
 * ## Configuration
 *
 * * `requestHelper` - a helper which will perform requests. `REST` by default, also `Playwright` or `GraphQL` can be used. Custom helpers must have `onResponse` hook in their config, which will be executed when request is performed.
 *
 * ### Examples
 *
 * Zero-configuration when paired with REST:
 *
 * ```js
 * // inside codecept.conf.js
 *{
 *   helpers: {
 *     REST: {
 *       endpoint: 'http://site.com/api',
 *     },
 *     JSONResponse: {}
 *   }
 *}
 * ```
 * Explicitly setting request helper if you use `makeApiRequest` of Playwright to perform requests and not paired REST:
 *
 * ```js
 * // inside codecept.conf.js
 * // ...
 *   helpers: {
 *     Playwright: {
 *       url: 'https://localhost',
 *       browser: 'chromium',
 *     },
 *     JSONResponse: {
 *       requestHelper: 'Playwright',
 *     }
 *   }
 * ```
 * ## Access From Helpers
 *
 * If you plan to add custom assertions it is recommended to create a helper that will retrieve response object from JSONResponse:
 *
 *
 * ```js
 * // inside custom helper
 * const response = this.helpers.JSONResponse.response;
 * ```
 *
 * ## Methods
 */
class JSONResponse extends Helper {
  constructor(config = {}) {
    super(config)
    this.options = {
      requestHelper: 'REST',
    }
    this.options = { ...this.options, ...config }
  }

  _beforeSuite() {
    this.response = null
    if (!this.helpers[this.options.requestHelper]) {
      throw new Error(`Error setting JSONResponse, helper ${this.options.requestHelper} is not enabled in config, helpers: ${Object.keys(this.helpers)}`)
    }
    const origOnResponse = this.helpers[this.options.requestHelper].config.onResponse;
    this.helpers[this.options.requestHelper].config.onResponse = response => {
      this.response = response;
      if (typeof origOnResponse === 'function') origOnResponse(response);
    };
  }

cd /testbed && node -e "
const REST = require('./lib/helper/REST');
const JSONResponse = require('./lib/helper/JSONResponse');
const Container = require('./lib/container');

// Simulate the user's scenario
const config = {
    helpers: {
        REST: {
            endpoint: 'https://httpbin.org',
            onRequest: (request) => {
                console.log('onRequest called');
                request.headers['start-time'] = Date.now();
            },
            onResponse: (response) => {
                console.log('onResponse called - user callback');
                console.log('Response status:', response.status);
            }
        },
        JSONResponse: {}
    }
};

// Create container
Container.create(config);

const restHelper = Container.helpers('REST');
const jsonHelper = Container.helpers('JSONResponse');

console.log('Before _beforeSuite:');
console.log('  restHelper.config.onResponse:', typeof restHelper.config.onResponse);

// Simulate what happens when suite starts - call _beforeSuite
jsonHelper._beforeSuite();

console.log('After _beforeSuite:');
console.log('  restHelper.config.onResponse:', typeof restHelper.config.onResponse);

// Now make a request
(async () => {
    const response = await restHelper.sendGetRequest('/get');
    console.log('\\nAfter request:');
    console.log('  jsonHelper.response:', jsonHelper.response ? 'set' : 'null');
    console.log('Done');
})().catch(err => console.error(err));

🎬 ACTION
cd /testbed && node -e "
const REST = require('./lib/helper/REST');
const JSONResponse = require('./lib/helper/JSONResponse');
const Container = require('./lib/container');

// Simulate the user's scenario
const config = {
    helpers: {
        REST: {
            endpoint: 'https://httpbin.org',
            onRequest: (request) => {
                console.log('onRequest called');
                request.headers['start-time'] = Date.now();
            },
            onResponse: (response) => {
                console.log('onResponse called - user callback');
                console.log('Response status:', response.status);
            }
        },
        JSONResponse: {}
    }
};

// Create container
global.codeceptjs = require('./lib');
Container.create(config);

const restHelper = Container.helpers('REST');
const jsonHelper = Container.helpers('JSONResponse');

console.log('Before _beforeSuite:');
console.log('  restHelper.config.onResponse:', typeof restHelper.config.onResponse);

// Simulate what happens when suite starts - call _beforeSuite
jsonHelper._beforeSuite();

console.log('After _beforeSuite:');
console.log('  restHelper.config.onResponse:', typeof restHelper.config.onResponse);

// Now make a request
(async () => {
    const response = await restHelper.sendGetRequest('/get');
    console.log('\\nAfter request:');
    console.log('  jsonHelper.response:', jsonHelper.response ? 'set' : 'null');
    console.log('  response.status:', response.status);
    console.log('Done');
})().catch(err => console.error(err));

The fix works. Both the user's `onResponse` and JSONResponse's internal handler are called.

But wait, I should also check if there's a similar issue in the REST helper itself. Let me look at the REST helper more carefully. The `onRequest` and `onResponse` are accessed via `this.config`. But what if someone sets them via `this.options`? Let me check if there's inconsistency.

Actually, looking at the REST constructor:

```js
constructor(config) {
    super(config)  // this.config = config
    this.options = {
        timeout: 10000,
        defaultHeaders: {},
        endpoint: '',
        prettyPrintJson: false,
        onRequest: null,
        onResponse: null,
    }
    // ...
    this._setConfig(config)  // this.options = config (overwrites the above)
```

So after construction:
- `this.config` = original config
- `this.options` = original config (same object)

They're the same object! Because `_setConfig` just does `this.options = this._validateConfig(opts)` which returns `opts` unchanged. So both point to the same object.

But the JSONResponse helper modifies `this.helpers[...].config.onResponse`. Since `config` and `options` are the same object, this also affects `options`. So my fix should work.

Now let me also check if there's another edge case. What if the user only has REST without JSONResponse? Then the original `onResponse` works fine. What if they have JSONResponse without a custom `onResponse`? Then `origOnResponse` would be undefined and the new callback just sets `this.response`. That's fine.

Let me also check if `onRequest` has any similar issues in other helpers. Let me search for where `onRequest` is overwritten:
